title: ginkgo status improvements
body:
a series of improvements to our ginkgo test framework so we can
get better ideas of whats going on when run in CI

Signed-off-by: baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *baude* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [baude] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Took a peek, posted some questions.  Only major concern is that new timeout-loop waiting for a container count.  Seems like that could race against other parallel tests starting/stopping containers.

Otherwise in general, running tests in parallel makes me slightly nervous.  Despite doing your best to synchronize resources, it's never going to be perfect.  Since the test-order is also randomized, this can lead to obscure, order-dependent AND resource-dependent flakes.

OTOH, I don't have many good solutions for that which also let the tests run really frickin' fast :smile: 

Off the top of my head...best I got is:  Have the random seed be set based on the day of the week.  At least that way, if something only fails on Tuesdays, we'll have somewhat of a clue as to the cause :smile:

okay, let's give this a spin in the wild-west, what's the worst that could happen? We'll have to do more work, at work? :smile: 

/lgtm
